cinematic_pitch: Carve through a labyrinth of mountain passes where asphalt snakes beneath towering peaks that pierce the Pacific Northwest sky. Each mile reveals a rugged symphony of elevation and precision, demanding focus and rewarding riders with breathtaking, unyielding vistas that redefine alpine adventure.
ride_character: An advanced mountain asphalt experience, the ride challenges with steep, technical passes framed by jagged summits and relentless elevation shifts, creating an intense and immersive rhythm.
best_season: June through September — the passes shed their snow and the weather stabilizes for optimal traction and visibility amid the high alpine terrain.

premium_pois.name: Mount Garibaldi
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Dominating the British Columbia skyline at 2,678 meters, Mount Garibaldi's rugged silhouette offers riders a dramatic backdrop that epitomizes the untamed spirit of the Pacific Northwest.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Check weather conditions before heading near Garibaldi, as sudden mountain shifts can affect visibility and road grip.

premium_pois.name: Mount Tantalus
premium_pois.cinematic_description: At 2,608 meters, Mount Tantalus looms with steep granite faces that descend sharply towards the winding asphalt, inviting riders to test their precision against nature’s towering fortress.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Approach the passes near Mount Tantalus with caution; their technical demands reward careful throttle and brake modulation.

premium_pois.name: The Sphinx
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Rising to 2,402 meters, The Sphinx commands attention with its enigmatic presence, framing the ride with an austere grandeur that heightens every twist and turn through the mountain corridors.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Take advantage of clear days to pause and absorb the panoramic views here—roadside pullouts provide safe spots for breaks.
